channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — Officials were investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead waited anxiously Saturday for answers.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Phoenix Mercury coach Nate Tibbetts ejected from WNBA Finals Game 4 after heated argument
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Phoenix Mercury coach Nate Tibbetts was ejected from Game 4 of the WNBA Finals after receiving two quick technical fouls in the third quarter against the Las Vegas Aces on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT and DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, Fox 5

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, AstraZeneca unveil drug pricing breaks for US consumers
item.description: The president said the U.K.-based company will list many of its most popular drugs online at TrumpRx, a new website.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started, an attempt by President Donald Trump's administration to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown dragged into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Connecticut man, woman charged with 31 counts of animal cruelty after dead animals found in trailer, bus
item.description: "That's crazy, man," Julius Bogues, who lives in Waterbury, said. "We ride by here and you never know what's going on right around you every day, very bizarre.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, NewsNation

item.title: Man convicted of killing a 15-year-old girl in her home in 2001 is executed by injection in Indiana
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was executed by injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year. Roy Lee Ward, 53, was put to death at the Indiana State Prison in Michigan City.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Did Trump end 7 wars? What to know ahead of Nobel Peace Prize announcement
item.description: President Trump's moment of truth as a global peacemaker will come early Friday morning when the Nobel Peace Prize is awarded. Trump has made no secret of his desire to win the prize.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown. "The RIFs have begun," Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Director Russ Vought posted on X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he looking at 'massive increase' in tariffs against China
item.description: President Donald Trump says “there seems to be no reason” to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping as part of an upcoming trip to South Korea after China restricted exports of rare earths needed for American industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is rolling out a new video of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown,
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators urge RFK Jr. to crack down on medication abortion
item.description: Almost every Senate Republican on Thursday urged the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to “reevaluate” whether a newly approved generic version of the abortion drug mifepristone should stay on the market. The letter to Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Bryan Kohberger caught on camera calmly shopping at stores hours after Idaho student murders
item.description: Surveillance video shows Bryan Kohberger shopping hours after killing four University of Idaho students before his guilty plea resulted in life sentences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico's president says Trump open to individual trade deals instead of USMCA
item.description: "There might be revisions that create bilateral deals instead of involving the three countries because some things are more important between Mexico and the United Sates or between Canada and the United States," said Sheinbaum. "Not everything has to be trilateral.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey businessman who testified against ex-Sen. Bob Menendez won't go to prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New Jersey businessman who testified against former Democratic Sen. Bob Menendez and his wife at separate bribery trials won’t go to prison, after a judge credited him at his sentencing Thursday for showing honesty on the witness stand and sincere remorse.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina Rep. Cecil Brockman accused of statutory rape with 15-year-old victim, warrants allege; Medical emergency delays court appearance
item.description: GUILFORD COUNTY, N.C. (WGHP) -- A North Carolina State House of Representatives member who represents Guilford County has been arrested and charged with multiple sex crimes.
item.dc:creator: Emily Mikkelsen, NewsNation

item.title: St. Petersburg to vote on flags for LGBTQ+ and Black communities after DeSantis erases murals
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — The Florida city of St. Petersburg, long a bastion of LGBTQ+ rights and mindful of its racially segregated past, is considering approval of two flags recognizing those issues after Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: From rubble to revival, Detroiters hope new Hudson's development can help reshape the city
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Before the arrival of sprawling suburban malls featuring amusement park rides and stores peddling anything from jeans to jelly beans, there was Hudson's in downtown Detroit.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Frustrated lawmakers say lack of trust is making it harder to end the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president looking to seize power beyond the executive branch. A Congress controlled by Republican lawmakers unwilling to directly defy him. And a minority party looking for any way to fight back.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge sets Nov. 14 deadline for new prosecutor in Georgia election case against Trump
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge says that the agency tasked with finding a prosecutor to take over the Georgia election interference case against President Donald Trump and others can have a little more time, but not as much time as it wanted.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jail's tight leash on Charlie Kirk's alleged assassin all about 'safety,' not silencing, expert says
item.description: Former federal prison official explains why the Utah jail isolating alleged Charlie Kirk assassin Tyler Robinson is acting out of safety, not punishment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Last of 10 New Orleans jail escapees from May is captured under a house in Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A monthslong search for the only Louisiana inmate still on the run after an audacious May jailbreak ended Wednesday when authorities say they found him hiding in a basement crawl space under an Atlanta home, bringing the last of the 10 escapees into custody.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CDC stops recommending COVID-19 shots for all, leaves decision to patients
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has adopted recommendations by a new group of vaccine advisers, and stopped recommending COVID-19 shots for anyone — leaving the choice up to patients.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4
